summary: Kenneth documents his weekly covered call selling performance across multiple stocks (HIMS, OLO, JOBY, Nphase), achieving $1,133 in premiums on a $27,455 deployed capital for a 4.13% weekly return. He emphasizes accepting 'good enough' returns rather than chasing maximum profits, using a diversified single-option approach across 4-5 positions rather than the $50k Premium Desk model.
takeaways: Target strikes just above purchase price to ensure assignment (e.g., bought HIMS at $50.38, sold $50-51 calls)
Aim for 4-6.2% weekly premium returns on covered calls as acceptable performance
Accept assignment and redeploy capital weekly rather than holding for larger moves
Extend expiration to next week (7+ days) when premiums are exceptionally high (Nphase example at 6.2%)
Buying after strong rallies is high-risk but acceptable if premium income justifies the risk
Deploy capital across multiple positions simultaneously (4-5 stocks in one week) rather than concentrating on single stock
Philosophy: 'Your goal isn't to make all the money, just some of it' - recognize good enough returns to avoid regret

strategy_candidates.change: Expand from single-stock HIMS focus to intentional 4-5 stock rotation weekly, maintaining $25-27k deployment across OLO, JOBY, Nphase, HIMS positions
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth successfully rotated into 4 different stocks this week, generating $1,133 in diversified premiums vs. struggling to hit $180 target on HIMS alone; demonstrated higher consistency with portfolio approach

strategy_candidates.change: Reserve capital for 7+ day expiration calls when IV is elevated and premiums exceed 5.5% weekly (vs. standard weekly cycle)
strategy_candidates.evidence: Nphase position at 6.2% return with next-Friday expiration outperformed weekly calls; Kenneth explicitly noted 'premiums were really high' as reason to extend duration

title: 7/18 part 7.5
